comment_text.sn33.51:1.7: This is the only occurrence in the early canon of paccakkha (“directly experience”). It went on to become a key term in Buddhist epistemology. The schools recognized that there are two forms of valid knowledge—direct experience and inference (anumāna)—and differed in the explanations.

root_text.sn33.51:1.2: Atha kho vacchagotto paribbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn33.51:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho vacchagotto paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn33.51:1.4: “ko nu kho, bho gotama, hetu, ko paccayo, yānimāni anekavihitāni diṭṭhigatāni loke uppajjanti—
root_text.sn33.51:1.6: neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti vā”ti?
root_text.sn33.51:1.7: “Rūpe kho, vaccha, appaccakkhakammā, rūpasamudaye appaccakkhakammā, rūpanirodhe appaccakkhakammā, rūpanirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāya appaccakkhakammā …pe….
root_text.sn33.52:1.2: “Vedanāya kho, vaccha, appaccakkhakammā …pe… vedanānirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāya appaccakkhakammā …pe….
root_text.sn33.53:1.2: “Saññāya kho, vaccha, appaccakkhakammā …pe… saññānirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāya appaccakkhakammā …pe….
root_text.sn33.54:1.2: “Saṅkhāresu kho, vaccha, appaccakkhakammā …pe… saṅkhāranirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāya appaccakkhakammā …pe….

translation_text.sn33.51:0.3: Four Discourses on Not Directly Experiencing Form, Etc.
translation_text.sn33.51:1.2: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta went up to the Buddha and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn33.51:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn33.51:1.4: “What is the cause, worthy Gotama, what is the reason why these various misconceptions arise in the world? …”
translation_text.sn33.51:1.7: “Vaccha, it is because of not directly experiencing form …
